summary:
Great! The test has passed, indicating that our fix for the deadlock issue was successful. By ensuring that the locks are always acquired in a consistent order based on account IDs, we've eliminated the possibility of a deadlock occurring during concurrent fund transfers.

To summarize, the issue was that the original code locked the accounts in parameter order (from, then to), which could lead to a deadlock when two concurrent transfers went in opposite directions (A->B and B->A). Our fix ensures that the locks are always acquired in a consistent order, regardless of the direction of the transfer, by locking the account with the lower ID first. This prevents the deadlock from occurring and allows the concurrent transfers to complete successfully.
